How do you find the right insights?
Go back to first principles.
You talk to the people that you're trying to serve.
You understand them deeply, more deeply than anyone else.
You will go learn their businesses better than anyone else.
You go and learn their day-to-day better than anyone else.
You go figure out what questions they're asking, right?

Where are they confused?

Like, they're all the same questions.

Were you always like a technical SEO guy or like what's your background?
No, I never was never an SEO.
I started my career at IBM and one of the first things I noticed when I was there, we were spending several million dollars in content syndication and it was the lowest ROI thing we had.
It was so expensive, was garbage, the leads were horrible, it didn't convert.
But then you just had these old school marketers that were measured on vanity metrics like leads.
And so they kept investing there.

And so I was just so annoyed because I was managing one of our blogs and all the content I was getting from people was product update, what features were launched for this product, version 8.7, you know, V2 or whatever, right?
And it's like, what the hell is this?
This is so garbage.

So as a new in this, for SEO purposes and to optimize for SEO, like AI and with all this, is this something different than traditional SEO to optimize for like as far as like technical things?
Just really short.
Is it different or is it pretty much the same?
I think what's different is how to approach it where you're, like a lot of companies, they're losing click throughs on AI answers.
And so they need to create more surface area and different surface area because they need to go more personalized, more long tail.
And the main reason for that is because like the best corporate credit cards to buy, right?
Like those are like the really sought after.
The second Google turns on AI searches, you're F, right?
You just lost 80% of your $20 a click queries that you took years to earn the first spot at, right?
So now all of a sudden you just lost 80% of your most valuable traffic without losing any rank, right?
And so now you just need to find a lot of different angles to come at.
And I think the tactics are you need to figure out how to convert your audience better into subscribers so that you own your destiny, right?

And whether that's like having a newsletter, like a podcast, whether it's being social, like how you own that audience more so that like you can control when you post, what you say, when you say it, and how it's going to get in front of people more often versus waiting for them to think about how to search for you, right?

And then overall, it's just going to like even out because the main thing I like to tell people is all these big players that are the traffic controllers of the web, the Google, OpenAI, Perplexity Now, they have no incentive to remove the incentive.
It's for people to have websites in the world, right?
And so what that means is like, if there's no incentive for people to have websites, there would be no incentive for people to share information.
If there's no incentive for people to share information or for businesses to be on the web, then they're not spending money on advertisement.
Advertisement still drives a majority of the ads.

it's like, there's no way the future looks like a monopoly of one player controlling 100% of the traffic on the web and no more websites in the planet, which is like, some people think it's going that way, right?
